summary: Go-based test orchestration framework with modular runner, schema validation, reporting, and MCP integration, developed through a multi-agent workflow system
patterns: modular internal packages (runner, schema, reporter, mcp)
concerns: heavy test proliferation across many agent directories may indicate fragmented ownership
assertion_wire_test.go suggests test coupling or shared test infrastructure
multiple parallel test suites (connectivity, prometheus, schema, goss) could lead to maintenance burden
agent metadata commits interleaved with feature commits suggest automated workflow noise
suggestions: consolidate overlapping test suites under unified test packages
document the GOrchestra agent orchestration strategy and naming conventions
add integration test coverage for MCP handlers
consider extracting shared assertion utilities to reduce duplication
establish commit conventions to separate metadata from functional changes
